top_reviews.author: Nik
top_reviews.text: India Grocers, located in Farmington Hills, is an excellent Indian grocery store. I have visited multiple times over the last year and have not been disappointed! The store is large and has an excellent selection of items. There is a large assortment of fresh fruits and vegetables. The store also has a good assortment of other items including snacks, spices, rice, frozen items, food items, cookware, and more. I have been able to find some of my favorite snacks here that I have never seen at other Indian grocery stores. During Diwali, there is a large selection of ready-to-eat sweets. The store is well-organized and items are well-stocked. There is ample parking outside. Overall, India Grocers is an excellent Indian Grocery store with a large variety of items.

top_reviews.author: ROOPANJALI KILLARU
top_reviews.text: I’ve seen many out dated products that are still being sold in the store . It’s not just 1 or 2 products but I’ve seen almost 5 to 6 products that are out dated . I’ve seen one from 2024 which is insane , I think this is smthng that everyone should be aware of .

top_reviews.author: sahithi uppaluru
top_reviews.text:
I would love to give zero for this store but Google doesn’t allow you to do that.
We’ve been living in the U.S. for the past 40 years, right here in Michigan. We usually shop at Costco or Walmart, but for specific Indian groceries, we’ve supported this store for years.

Yesterday, while we were waiting in the checkout line, a young student politely asked about a price difference on a Thumbs Up bottle. He mentioned he had bought it for 0.99 before, and now it showed 1.47. Instead of handling it with respect or checking the system, the manager immediately accused him of lying in front of everyone. He didn’t even bother to ask his employees or verify the price in their system.

Today, when my husband stopped by to grab some coriander, he happened to see the same student again. The boy told my husband that he had shown his receipt proving it was indeed 0.99, and the manager, instead of apologizing, got scared and brushed it off, saying “Fine, just this one time we’ll give it for 0.99 but from next time it’s 1.47,” and then ran away. Moments later, the same manager came out again after making sure the kid had left the store.

This kind of behavior is absolutely unacceptable. No customer deserves to be treated like that, especially a young student just asking a simple question. That manager should either be removed or taught how to treat people with basic respect.

After witnessing this, we’re deeply disappointed and honestly disturbed by the arrogance and lack of decency shown. This is the dark side of how some people in management treat others when they think no one’s watching.

Attaching the receipt picture my husband received from the student as proof.

top_reviews.author: Rakesh Leeladhar
top_reviews.text:
I always go to Vani stores. Recently, I got 2 coconuts and 1 of them was bad. I went back to return them and get another instead. The person in the front was rude and mentioned,  there are no refunds or exchange on coconuts and he said 50% coconuts they get is bad and hence there are 2 different pricing. If you break them open at the store its higher price, if you take it home without breaking them at the store its a lower price. Basically, they are taking chances to use your money in their favor by selling coconuts,  even though they know they buy 50% rotten coconuts.

They either need to change their distributor or take care of their customers.

India grocers are not far away from them and I'm taking my business there moving forwards.

They need to learn how to take care of their customers.

top_reviews.author: Sha
top_reviews.text:
This is one of the busiest and most popular stores in town, and it’s easy to see why! They offer consistently fresh vegetables and an excellent variety of freshly made snacks like vadas, pakoras, and veg puffs. The staff is helpful and efficient, making every visit smooth and pleasant.

It’s a one-stop shop for all grocery needs, with an impressive selection of products across categories. During Navratri season, they also had a wonderful variety of Golu items, which added to the festive shopping experience.

The store is clean, well-organized, and conveniently located with ample parking. It’s open every day of the week, making it super accessible.

However, the narrow aisles can feel a bit cramped during peak hours, and the lack of price labels on vegetables can be confusing. Despite these minor issues, this store remains a go-to for authentic Indian groceries and fresh produce. Highly recommended!

top_reviews.author: sowmya sivaraman
top_reviews.text: Extreme rude customer service . Have been here so many times , but looks like they have too many new customers and do not care if the old ones want to keep coming. For the 5 items I had , I requested a plastic bag instead of the box ( can’t carry because of shoulder injury ) she was rude and snubbed me and told me I should carry a bag . I tried to explain it was unplanned and have an injury . The bag she was giving me was torn and when I requested a new one - she started snubbing me and said I should go to other stores . Thank you !

top_reviews.author: GOD IS GREAT
top_reviews.text:
Highest priced Indian grocery store compared to all other Indian grocery stores in south east Michigan. I went to 3 different Indian stores after visiting Vani foods , they are charging $1 to 3 $ more for  same products(same brand)  than all other 3 Indian stores I visited. I have been purchasing from Vani foods last 15 years and yesterday was my last day visiting this store.
Also now this stores have lot of improperly labeled items displayed and when they scan it will a different.
Also lots of of arrogant unprofessional employees with small aisles that cannot be maneuvered safely .
I will be writing to the state general attorney’s office and FTC  about this greedy price gouging .

top_reviews.author: priest madhavan
top_reviews.text: I bought banana leaves 10 today. I requested the lady before packing make sure the banana leaves are good and I need only good leaves. She packed and gave it to me. I opened in front of the lady 4 leaves are torn . I returned immediately. I asked the price she told 2.99 $. But they charged 3.49 $. Make sure the price and the materials before you buy. Thanks

I highly recommend this store to anyone looking for quality Indian groceries and delicious fresh snacks!

top_reviews.author: Mr. Allen
top_reviews.text: I absolutely LOVE this place. The samosas are delicious and very reasonably priced. The customer service is always above board. They actually treat you like family and will help you without making you feel like a moron. I will alway come here for any snacks and sauces!

top_reviews.author: Mari B
top_reviews.text: Just went here today and it was very clean and organized. It looks small but they have everything you need. The owner Shiva is very nice and genuine guy. I will be coming here for my south Asian groceries from now on!!!! The samosas are soooo yummy!!! The chai is good too!

top_reviews.author: mariya wood
top_reviews.text: This place is hands down the crown jewel of South Asian grocery shopping. Walking in, you’re instantly hit with the warm  smell  of fresh spices that feels like home. The aisles are packed with everything you could possibly want. But what really makes this place shine is the customer service. The staff don’t just help you find things, they actually know the products, give genuine recommendations, and treat you like family. It doesn’t feel like a quick errand, it feels like an experience. Prices are unbeatable for the quality you’re getting.

top_reviews.author: Rayyan Sharief
top_reviews.text:
If I could give Desi Mart a million stars I would because five stars doesn’t even come close to doing this place justice. Walking into Desi Mart is like stepping into heaven on earth. The second those doors open you are hit with a wave of freshness, color, and life that makes you wonder if you’ve been shopping in the wrong places your whole life. This is not just a grocery store. This is a sanctuary of flavor, culture, warmth, and community.

The produce shines like it has been picked just for you that morning. Every fruit and vegetable looks like it belongs in a magazine spread. The spices are not just stocked, they are glowing. You can feel the energy from the vibrant reds, yellows, and greens of the masalas, the rich depth of whole cardamoms and cloves, the comforting warmth of cinnamon sticks, and the fiery promise of chili powders. Walking through the aisles you get this feeling in your chest that you are not only shopping, you are reconnecting with tradition, family, and authenticity.

And the staff. My God, the staff. They don’t just smile, they welcome you like you are family coming home after a long trip. They know what they are talking about, they know how to guide you to exactly what you need, and they care in a way that feels rare these days. Every interaction feels genuine, thoughtful, and kind. You walk out feeling lighter, not just because you found everything on your list but because you felt seen and appreciated.

The variety here is endless. Everything you thought you would have to visit multiple stores for is right here under one roof. Fresh produce, frozen goods, snacks that remind you of home, and new treasures waiting to be discovered. Prices are honest and fair, which makes it even more unbelievable considering the quality. This store was clearly built with love and with the goal of serving the community at the highest level.

Desi Mart is not just a place to shop. It is a place that feeds your soul. It is a celebration of culture. It is proof that when people care enough to do something right, the result can be magical. I left the store with more than groceries. I left with happiness, with gratitude, and with excitement to come back again and again.

Farmington Hills does not know how lucky it is to have this gem. Desi Mart is a crown jewel, a place that elevates everyday life and turns the simple act of shopping into a joyful journey. If you have not been yet, drop what you are doing, grab your car keys, and go. This is not just a grocery store, this is the best place in the world.

top_reviews.author: Wasim Patel
top_reviews.text: Visited this new store, pricing is reasonable and they have all the needed products. Glad to see new store close to my house.

Very authentic Thirunveli sweets and snacks.  And food items too.  Made fresh, with home made taste. .  Portion sizes are very good.

Many organic items and traditional grocery items are available at the store.

top_reviews.author: S babu
top_reviews.text:
Wow we really fortunate enough to have this store.
Most of the stores carry regular grocery they have  no chemical pesticides lentils and rice bags etc ..
We liked it so much owner is very friendly he is taking time to explain the information well. Interestingly we have millet batters I haven’t seen these many verity millet batters earlier.
Price is bit expensive I agree with all others remember one thing. Quality is always costly.
You can’t expect $3 burger with quality and healthy 😀😃

top_reviews.author: Sakthi Rajan
top_reviews.text:
Food Quality is awesome and Customer service is great.

I love their tea and snacks, I heard from gentleman they are importing the tea products directly from Ooty estate. Good taste. Also, Sugar they provide is mixed with dry ginger which enhancing the tea flavor.

Onion samosa, Peanut Candy and Karuppu Kavuni rice payasam are must try.

My one stop shop for rice, oil & millet varieties.

top_reviews.author: Vidhya Elango
top_reviews.text:
I recently tried Desika Foods and had an overall awesome experience! They stock ingredients directly from Tamil Nadu and grind their own oil here, which really shows in the quality. The dosa batter, both millet and normal, was fantastic, and the dosas turned out amazing. The peanut oil they use has a great smell and addictive quality.

However, the prices are a bit on the higher side. On the downside, the person at the desk could have been more kind and polite - their attitude was off-putting.

Despite the price and service, I'd still highly recommend Desika Foods for their delicious and authentic food stuffs.

top_reviews.author: Linda williams
top_reviews.text:
We had a moderate experience with desika foods.we got non veg kothu porota.i dint like it and it was very dry and the gravy which they gave was not even appetizing!!expected the food will be like eating from our home town since they r from thirunelveli but disappointed with their food!

Also got their thirunelveli halwa which I dint feel so fresh and the sugar was crystallized but the taste way okay!

They are quite expensive too comparing other grocery shops!but u could see some items which we cannot get in any other grocery store.

top_reviews.author: Samragyee Pandey
top_reviews.text:
⚠️ PRICE SCAMMERS
They display one price when you add items to cart, then CHARGE YOU A DIFFERENT PRICE at checkout. The Maggi noodles and Cadbury chocolate showed specific prices in cart, but the actual charge? Completely inflated. Same exact products, same quantities, weights & sizes. I ordered through DoorDash.
THIS HAS HAPPENED 3 SEPARATE TIMES across different orders on different days. Evidently, it's their fraudulent business model. When I called to ask why, their response: "That's how it is, and don't buy from us if you have a problem." They have the audacity to say this!

This is evidently deceptive pricing and systematic fraud. Even the strategic store naming seems to be done to attract a customer base they wouldn't get otherwise. AVOID ⚠️

#UPDATE: Their response literally CONFIRMS the scam; they admitted prices "differ between cart and checkout" then tried to justify it with "platform fees." NO business does that except them. If you're charging more, show it UPFRONT in the cart, not at checkout. I'm not alone, another reviewer called out the exact same pricing tactic @ store. When I called them about it, their response was "That's how it is, don't buy from us if you have a problem." 0 accountability. Instead of fixing the issue, they responded to my review by attacking my character, victim-blaming me for ordering multiple times, threatening me about my "review pattern," Classic intimidation and gaslighting. Same products, mysteriously inflating prices. Screenshots and receipts don't lie. Other customers deserve this warning.

top_reviews.author: Brenda Vaishnav
top_reviews.text: Clean, organized, and friendly. Many new brands and varieties I haven’t seen at other Indian grocery stores. Large frozen foods area. Small fresh foods area. Nice ice cream area too!  I found a pistachio kulfi ice cream cone that was delicious. Fun grocery store! Store expansion in progress. Nice beauty area for the ladies.

top_reviews.author: Sharat Kumar
top_reviews.text: Decent place, very clean, big store and responsive staff, almost all the items are available here from South Indian snacks to household things, groceries everything, but prices can be little bit less. Paying by cash gives a 3% discount on total.

top_reviews.author: Shah Darshan
top_reviews.text: The sweets purchased from frozen sections all had fungi on them. I tried calling and sent email yet no response anywhere. Would rather prefer some other Indian grocery store from bunch of available options.

top_reviews.text:
**Nice Store**
New Indian store in Plymouth neighborhood. Near by Joy and Ann Arbor communities.
Items are cheaper compared to other Indian stores in Canton.
There are lots of offer going on now. Every alternative days store is offering fresh vegetables.

top_reviews.text: Gross Jalebi box. Smelled off as soon as I opened it. Tasted even worse. Had to throw away the box as it is. NO DATE CODE..!!!, but I can tell it's old. I don't think they will take it off the shelves as long as it looks okay. Going by previous comments, no point in trying to provide the feedback. Never buy anything without date code from this store. DONT BUY SWEETS from here..!!! Better stores in the area, don't risk your health.

top_reviews.author: Nik
top_reviews.text: India Grocers, located in Farmington Hills, is an excellent Indian grocery store. I have visited multiple times over the last year and have not been disappointed! The store is large and has an excellent selection of items. There is a large assortment of fresh fruits and vegetables. The store also has a good assortment of other items including snacks, spices, rice, frozen items, food items, cookware, and more. I have been able to find some of my favorite snacks here that I have never seen at other Indian grocery stores. During Diwali, there is a large selection of ready-to-eat sweets. The store is well-organized and items are well-stocked. There is ample parking outside. Overall, India Grocers is an excellent Indian Grocery store with a large variety of items.

top_reviews.author: ROOPANJALI KILLARU
top_reviews.text: I’ve seen many out dated products that are still being sold in the store . It’s not just 1 or 2 products but I’ve seen almost 5 to 6 products that are out dated . I’ve seen one from 2024 which is insane , I think this is smthng that everyone should be aware of .

top_reviews.author: sahithi uppaluru
top_reviews.text:
I would love to give zero for this store but Google doesn’t allow you to do that.
We’ve been living in the U.S. for the past 40 years, right here in Michigan. We usually shop at Costco or Walmart, but for specific Indian groceries, we’ve supported this store for years.

Yesterday, while we were waiting in the checkout line, a young student politely asked about a price difference on a Thumbs Up bottle. He mentioned he had bought it for 0.99 before, and now it showed 1.47. Instead of handling it with respect or checking the system, the manager immediately accused him of lying in front of everyone. He didn’t even bother to ask his employees or verify the price in their system.

Today, when my husband stopped by to grab some coriander, he happened to see the same student again. The boy told my husband that he had shown his receipt proving it was indeed 0.99, and the manager, instead of apologizing, got scared and brushed it off, saying “Fine, just this one time we’ll give it for 0.99 but from next time it’s 1.47,” and then ran away. Moments later, the same manager came out again after making sure the kid had left the store.

This kind of behavior is absolutely unacceptable. No customer deserves to be treated like that, especially a young student just asking a simple question. That manager should either be removed or taught how to treat people with basic respect.

After witnessing this, we’re deeply disappointed and honestly disturbed by the arrogance and lack of decency shown. This is the dark side of how some people in management treat others when they think no one’s watching.

Attaching the receipt picture my husband received from the student as proof.

top_reviews.author: Edsel Witkowski
top_reviews.text:
I want to say I really hate writing bad reviews, and I don't often do it unless I want to warn others. My girlfriend and I were in the mood for Pizzawala's (Canton, MI), but live in this area and wanted to stay in tonight.

We ordered a half and half pizza with Chicken Tikka and Chicken Masala and a Mango Lassi (drink). When picking up, the  guy at the counter asked if we wanted drizzle and cilantro which we said yes to.

We made it back home in 10 minutes and the pizza was luke warm. The chicken was very dry (to the point of picking it off). It tasted like it was cooked earlier and reheated. Part of the crust wasn't cooked on one slice and felt cold to the touch. One of the other reviewers said the pizza here is like "Little Caesars with chicken" and they aren't far off the mark. The pizza tastes like a frozen cheese pizza that they reheated and added reheated chicken to. The crust was floppy and soft.

Unfortunately the drink wasn't as good as the mango Lassi we normally order from restaurants. It tasted like Danimal's yogurt with mango flavoring. It was the only thing from the order we finished.

The drizzle and cilantro made the pizza somewhat edible but after a slice and a half each we couldn't bring ourselves to eat anymore. For $28.30 for a pizza ($21.99) and drink ($4.99) I can't recommend this place when a frozen pizza would do better. Pizzawala's large is only $20.99 and it's phenomenal.

top_reviews.author: Amir Kazemi
top_reviews.text:
If you’re looking for a true taste of India right here in town, look no further than this incredible Indian store. From the moment you walk in, it’s like stepping into a marketplace in Delhi or Mumbai. The selection is absolutely massive, and it’s clear that the store owners have gone out of their way to stock products that cater to the wide and diverse tastes of the Indian community—and even beyond.

The variety here is genuinely impressive. Whether you’re looking for pantry staples like basmati rice, lentils, flours (atta, besan, maida), or a wide array of spices and masalas.

There’s also an incredible range of frozen foods.

One thing I really appreciate is how well-organized and clean the store is. It’s not cluttered like some ethnic markets can be. Everything is categorized and labeled, making it easy to browse even if you’re not entirely familiar with all the items. The aisles are spacious, and the store is always tidy and well-lit.

They have great collection of goods and all vegetables are very fresh. Also the restaurant food is amazingly delicious with all vegetarian items. I’ll love to go back to get some more samosas.

top_reviews.author: Sha
top_reviews.text:
We were in Troy and decided to try this place based on a friend's recommendation, specifically for their pomfret. We called ahead to place an order over the phone. However, when we arrived for pickup after 30 minutes, we were told the order hadn't been prepared yet. That was disappointing, especially since we were told it will be ready in 20 minutes. The staff apologized and asked us to wait a few minutes, but we chose to leave and return later.

To their credit, they called us about five minutes before closing to let us know the order was ready and check if we were still coming. I appreciated that follow-up.

I was a bit hesitant at that point, not sure about the quality given the earlier mix-up, especially since the price was also lower than what we usually pay for pomfret.

But once we cooked it, wow! The quality of the fish was excellent and fresh. Definitely worth it in the end. Another plus, there is an Indian store right next door, so it's convenient if you're planning to shop while you wait or pick up other groceries.

If they can tighten up their order-handling process a bit, this would be a 5-star experience. For now, I'm giving 4 stars for the great product and their effort to make things right.

top_reviews.author: SeshiDhar Raghupathi
top_reviews.text:
I’m very disappointed with my experience. I bought chicken from this store, and while cleaning it at home, I found 2–3 Green muscle like spots inside the chicken. This raised serious concerns about hygiene and quality control.

When I contacted the store, I was asked to bring the product back for verification before they would consider a replacement. Given the nature of the issue, this response was frustrating and disappointing.

Food safety should not be treated this lightly. I expected better quality checks and more responsible customer service. Unfortunately, this experience has affected my trust in the store

top_reviews.author: Rudra Avatara
top_reviews.text: I am writing this after experiencing three separate incidents with chicken purchased from this famous meat shop. Each time, the quality was disappointing, and after the last one, my son started vomiting at school the next day. Please be very careful when buying chicken from here — I strongly recommend checking the freshness before purchasing.

top_reviews.author: V Sagar
top_reviews.text: I had a wonderful experience at Famous Meat Hub in Troy. Their wide range of Indian meat options, including prawns, impressed me. I especially liked how they customized the chicken cutting to suit different dishes like curry or biryani. The customer service was decent, adding to the overall positive visit.

top_reviews.author: Kevin Lore
top_reviews.text: Bought a $12.34 fish from meat hub it was rotten seller admitted it was a week old fish refused to refund me and wanted me to bring curry as proof.

top_reviews.author: Niha R
top_reviews.text:
A decent place for Indian eats with a very homely vibe. I loved the fact that everything felt comforting and familiar even the setup of the place adds to that feeling.
The menu is huge and impressive, starting from Osmania biscuits to dosas, idlis, thalis, biryanis, puffs, pakodas, bajjis, punugulu, and so much more  honestly, something for everyone.
I tried the mirchi bajji with onion stuffing and it was absolutely delicious. The punugulu with allam chutney were hands down the best. The chicken pakodas tasted exactly like the classic chicken starters from King & Cardinal, which used to be a very famous place in Hyderabad (now sadly shut down). Hyderabadis will totally understand what I mean pure nostalgia.
The samosas were yum too. For dessert, I had the falooda ice with crunchy sabja (basil seeds) and chikoo ice cream it was a feast.
Coming from Canada, it’s really hard to find food that matches our home flavors, but this place truly won my heart. Highly recommended for anyone craving authentic, homestyle Indian food.

top_reviews.author: maham mateen
top_reviews.text:
my husband and I went to get some quality ice cream; For one was very excited as I thought it would be classical desi Flavors.. I was a little disappointed overall as when I asked to taste the flavors. The lady was very sweet, However, the scoop of tasting was ever so small couldn’t even tell that was supposed to be sample size.

Overall, my husband and I both got two scoops of ice cream he got falooda And Dubai chocolate ice cream I have gotten the falooda And mango… I was really upset to see the ice cream did have freezer burn on it, which indicated it. It’s been there for not weeks but months yes you can store ice cream in the freezer for quite a while, but not months where it starts having freezer burn there was no flavor to the ice cream. We both were very disappointed and ended up throwing our full ice cream away after 4 to 5 bites of it and going to another ice cream shop… i thought i was crazy but my throat did hurt and to my surprise so did my husbands..

If they want to improve, they need to work on their KWALITY.

top_reviews.author: Mohit Balwwani
top_reviews.text:
If tea had a LinkedIn profile, Aahar Cafe & Kwality Ice Cream Troy’s - chai would be endorsed for excellence, consistency, and soul. This isn’t just tea—it’s a full-blown experience. Bold aroma, perfect kadak balance, zero shortcuts. Old-school method, modern execution. Respect.

Yes, they also have a location in Farmington Hills, Michigan—solid spot, no complaints. But let’s call a spade a spade: the best tea is hands down at the Troy location. Different league. Different vibe. That cup hits deeper, warmer, and somehow feels more intentional.

Bottom line:
•	Tea cravings? Solved.
•	Long day? Fixed.
•	Standards? Raised.

Aahar in Troy understands chai the way it’s always been meant to be made. No fluff. Just results. ☕🔥

top_reviews.author: somara divya
top_reviews.text:
I have visited Aahar Cafe of Farmington Hills for buffet couple of time and they have a very good spread with fresh food. Aahar Cafe at troy is relatively new and smaller with finger foods, ice creams, falooda's and regular indian menu with limited seating.

I usually go during weekends for their Non Veg and Veg Thali's. They provide ample amount of jeera rice along with roti, chicken starters curries, veg curries, papad and one type of sweet. Last weekend I have visited and was surprised to see that they have started buffet as well. They had a good spread a bit less than Farmington hills branch, but good enough. The price was approx 18 dollars per person. Will visit again for buffet.

top_reviews.author: ruthin rak
top_reviews.text:
After going for three times I’m writing here, tried bonda, punugulu, chicken fry, dosa, idli, vada, poori, tea. All the items tried were good.

Gone for lunch buffet on weekday, most of the items were good. I’m biryani lover, so when i tried butter chicken for nan, i see chicken is coloured. It is quite common in most of the restaurants they color for eye catching.

This is one of the safe place to try in troy for taste repeatedly

top_reviews.author: Neha
top_reviews.text: One of the most underrated restaurant. We’ve been there at least 50 times, and it has never disappointed us. Every dish is consistently on point and freshly made, which is something I can’t say about many other Indian restaurants in the area. The lunch special is an absolute delight, especially the dal, which tastes just like the one your mom would make at home. The staff is incredibly accommodating and always ensure your requests are met. Highly recommended!

top_reviews.author: Roland Feldmeier
top_reviews.text:
I have never been to India, but I have been to other parts of Asia. This location makes me feel like I am in one of those eateries in Asia. Nothing fancy, yet comfortable.

The photos of the different train stations in India are a nice touch.

I rate this restaurant for what it represents and not some outlandish dreams of grandeur of fine dining with waiters in tuxedos and gold tableware.

Mumbai Chowk is delicious home style cooking rich in flavors, colors, and smiles. The atmosphere is what I would expect from a family run restaurant in a small neighborhood of India, which is why I give the Atmosphere a 5-star rating.

While I have not tried every item on their menu, and I have eaten here multiple times, everything that I've eaten was delicious and fresh.

They have an assortment of naan, but hands down, they make THE best onion naan!!!

top_reviews.author: carmen mercado
top_reviews.text: I love the thali at dinner time.  It's filling and a very good price at $22 if paying by cash.  The 2 ladies at the front are friendly, quick, and nice too.

top_reviews.author: Rachana A
top_reviews.text:
This is the first time am trying this place , hands down to their best naans (butter/garlic) and sabudana vada , balti paneer, their Thalis have great options . We took the Bangda thali was good

We usually don’t prefer spicy food and this has to be our goto spot . Subtle flavors with decent spicy level . Overall I would give a 4/5 . Please keep the authenticity of the food same 😊

top_reviews.author: Nidhi Asthana
top_reviews.text:
Bad experience overall.
The tandoori chicken leg piece was burnt, extremely dry, and completely tasteless. The tandoori fish was equally disappointing,
there were very few pieces, and it lacked any flavor. When we shared this feedback, the lady at the counter responded arrogantly with just “thanks for your feedback,” without any concern or effort to address the issue. Definitely not the quality or service we expected.

top_reviews.author: Niel Rishoi
top_reviews.text: I am very glad this market has opened up in this part of town. The location is perfect, parking plentiful, and access is easy.  Little India is off to a fantastic start.  The store is clean, well-stocked, well-organized, and the family that owns it are exceptionally friendly and in helping me find items. The gentleman whom I spoke with even asked me what items I would like to see brought into the store. In the meantime, Little India has all the products that I need, that I cannot find in ordinary markets. I highly recommend this place enthusiastically.

top_reviews.author: Eileen Fields
top_reviews.text: Well, I took my daughter to the CVS in this same strip mall area to buy a Build-A-Bear via Facebook Marketplace and I had to use the bathroom.  I didn't think CVS had one so I went to Pande Grocers and they had no toilet paper inside the restroom.  When, I told a lady worker about it, she opened the door and pointed to the empty roll like I could use what little was Still on the roll! Uh, Nope! Then, a male worker came she told him what was needed, they both went into a storage room I think and came out to tell me that they Were Completely out of TP! I'm just wondering how she's going to use the bathroom with No TP? Hmmm...there was No sign saying that you had to buy something in order to use the facility, so what was the Real Reason?

Otherwise, it's a great place to shop.

top_reviews.author: Lauralyn Taylor
top_reviews.text:
The market is practically a mini Walmart with the range of products sold! It had pots, pans, rugs, nail polish, kitchen gadgets, glassware, and food that was American, Indian, Mexican and probably more. There was a nice selection of fresh vegetables and some fruits. There was also a refrigerated section with frozen vegetables, desserts, meals, and more. Milk, bread, butter, ghee, eggs... You name it, they had it (except thyme, lol, I didn't think they would have it, which makes sense, but since they had everything else, and I needed some, I looked 😊).

The store was clean and the employees were very helpful. I called before driving out (I live 10 miles away) and the employee was happy to tell me they carry fresh turmeric. When I arrived, he even took me back to where it is located.

top_reviews.author: Dewan Mahmud Ahmed
top_reviews.text: Billed for betel nuts but never received them. When I called immediately, the cashier responded irresponsibly and didn’t resolve it. Good selection, but poor service ruined the experience. Suggest no one to visit if it's not that much urgent.

top_reviews.author: John Alexander
top_reviews.text: Flavorful and authentic tasting food,  plus consistent quality, make this a good place if you like food from Kerala, or are curious to try dishes from this Southern India state. The non-vegetarian dishes are very good, and nothing is drowning in spices or oil. Available for takeout on WEEKENDS only-prices are competitive with similar quantities from restaurants

top_reviews.author: Gokulnadh NK
top_reviews.text:
Small store, accessible only from Ryan road.
Limited stocks of kerala dried foods and necessities available.. lot of frozen things were also available.. but not sure what good “Groceries” I could get..
People here were very friendly. But they pushed a lot of left over cold food with me and for expensive price.. I felt food here was expensive to other Indian foods around.. A small tray of Kappa and curry costed me over 20 bucks and didn’t even tasted good.. I had to add salt, spices and reheat to make it edible.. Fish biriyani I got here was not good either.. I would not recommend this place.. and don’t see any reason to go back there again..

top_reviews.author: Hannah Davis
top_reviews.text: This is authentic Kerala-style Indian food, and it is good. The proprietors were very gracious and willing to explain what different dishes were and how they were made. Diners are encouraged to eat in the traditional style (with your hands), but there was also silverware available to those who wanted it. The dining area attached to the grocery is quite small, but on Sundays they serve a buffet (though really, you sit at the table and the servers just bring you some of whatever is on the menu for that day) in the overflow space a few doors down from the grocery. I highly recommend!

top_reviews.author: Sruthi Sreedhar
top_reviews.text: Great place for Kerala food. They have the best Indian Buffet!! It's only available on Sundays but all the food items are absolutely delicious. Their biriyani is amazing along with their malabar chicken curry. Great food no doubt! This is my number 1 choice for Indian food.

top_reviews.text: Went to Bombay grocers.Its was much better than competitors. A Must visit store in Ann Arbor. Fresh produce also available.

top_reviews.author: Shon Lang
top_reviews.text:
My family and I have been coming to this store for years. They have been a staple for all our Indian grocery needs. The store is always clean, neat and organized really well. Staff is alwsys friendly and helpful. I always see alot of Indian and Asian people shopping here but I see alot of varieties of other cultures as well. I think it's nice to see people trying to get into Indian cooking and food. I've seen the owner and other workers helping and explaining to people who might be new to the culture and products.

They also have homemade rotis, Samosas, and Indian deserts.

top_reviews.author: Md Amaan
top_reviews.text:
Unlike other grocery stores in town this store has lots of different types of frozen food, groceries and fresh vegetable produce and they offer much cheap prizes on their products than their competitors while maintaining standards as I’ve been going to this store for buying groceries for more than a year but I didn’t find any expired products yet, hats off to the friendly and helpful store employees.

I recommend all of our desi community to  pay this store a visit at least once if they haven’t done yet so that you would know the difference (A little tip, they got lots of products which we cannot find in other Indian grocery stores).

top_reviews.author: Kathryn Friedlund
top_reviews.text:
Usually go to the Bombay in canton but come here when I'm close.  Much larger store than canton and has produce and I believe more freezer/cooler space.

Overall I love Bombay grocers for gluten free cooking and vegetarian cooking. Great values here for flours and spices! I like the snacks too and branching out to try new things.... I got sick once from gluten unknowing it was in one of the spice blends, so beware if you're celiac while trying new things.

top_reviews.author: Upsana Reddy
top_reviews.text: We are happy to hear the response! We are new to Ann Arbor. Definitely will be your regulars!

top_reviews.text:
Legit Indian food! Just beware that on weekends the price of buffet is $19.99, which is different than what they say on their website. Other wise good
At least for our palates. My family loves it. The tandoori chicken is some of the best. The buffet is reasonably priced and has a great selection.

top_reviews.author: Sara V.
top_reviews.text: Amazing first time experience! Highly recommend the chicken chili, chicken tikka masala, and the coffee—one of the best I’ve ever had!

top_reviews.author: _t.ba_
top_reviews.text: They have lunch buffet all week with weekdays being the cheapest at 14.99, then saturday 15.99 and then sunday special lunch buffet being the most expensive at 17.99. I went to the saturday buffet and it was delicious! Tikka masala and gobi 65 was very good and fresh. Would recommend for the price, but the atmosphere and restaurant is a little old, and has not as much variety as I would've liked.

top_reviews.author: Evan Stocker
top_reviews.text: Fantastic food and servers. The kitchen helped make dishes that fit my dietary restrictions. (Gluten free, dairy free). Would recommend! Also the space is very clean and peaceful.

top_reviews.author: Oshita S
top_reviews.text: Best Indian grocery store in Ann Arbor. I have been shopping here since 2019. Owner is very friendly and helpful. Has most Indian foods. Owner will order stuff for you if you don't find it in the store.

top_reviews.author: Jess Liang
top_reviews.text: This is the place where I get all my Indian groceries. The prices are great, and the man who's always working there is so nice! So glad it's in my area of town, because it's convenient to go there whenever I need something.

top_reviews.author: Alijah Jonah
top_reviews.text: Wonderful little shop that has everything I need to make some of my favorite Indian dishes at home. Service is always quick and friendly as well. Highly recommend checking them out!

top_reviews.author: Crescent Cundy
top_reviews.text: Wonderful!! Just stumbled upon this market and was so pleased with all of the different goodies. Great selection,  employees are so nice and even recommended some treats for us. Tried a variety of snacks for the first time and didn't pick a single thing we didn't love. We will be back for sure!!!!

top_reviews.author: Steve Pickard
top_reviews.text: Best prices and selection of the various A2 Indian food markets. I enjoy the owner and he's always helpful with advice and good vibe. i only go here now when I need my indian food ingredients for various instapot and home cooking projects.

name: Ajanta Grocers & Carry Out
specialties: Indian grocery with carry out food
formatted_address: 3227 South Blvd, Auburn Hills, MI 48326, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 11:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM

top_reviews.author: Joie Fuertez
top_reviews.text: This place has it all…there is such a wide array of choices. I picked up some frozen appetizers, spice mixes, drinks, and Indian snacks. If there’s any Indian grocery item you’re looking for, this store will have it!!!! I also ordered food from their kitchen, which was so fresh and delicious. The aloo chole and paneer biryani was amazingly flavorful. This is a family-owned business, and the staff are very caring and have a great passion for their store. Not to mention the chutneys and lunch special items are made fresh with love! Highly recommend :)

top_reviews.author: Sharat Kumar
top_reviews.text: Good store with all the household items available but less vegetables items.

top_reviews.author: Nick Velez
top_reviews.text: Best samosas I've ever had! Don't even need the mint. Fresh and delicious! Also, they have a great selection of everything else you could want or need to make your own food at home.

top_reviews.author: Amber Nyquist
top_reviews.text: Soooooooo tasty!! I love this place for groceries and takeout!! The prices are beyond reasonable! They have a lot of takeout specials including a lunch box that never disappoints. No meat required… these vegetarian dishes are SO FLAVORFUL!!

top_reviews.author: James Delpaz
top_reviews.text: Some of the best Indian food I’ve ever had. Great price and super friendly service the cashier walks you through the lunch items which I found really helpful. Would recommend anytime you’re in the area.

top_reviews.author: Sheikh in America
top_reviews.text: That’s Perfect Place For Pak\Ind Grocery. You can Buy All kind of our Desi food from here. Easily you can find everything well organised Store & Owner are very Polite and nice Recommend to visit here 👍🏼

top_reviews.author: Sleezy Hamilton
top_reviews.text: Clean, cheap, stocked with many options that are otherwise hard to find downriver. Staff was helpful and friendly.  Excellent

top_reviews.author: Abdul Monnan Jr.
top_reviews.text: Very nice selection of Indian and Pakistani food products! The storekeep was friendly and helpful. 👍

top_reviews.author: Maria Yousaf
top_reviews.text:
I got frozen naan from this place, when I opened the package the naan were moldy. I checked the expiration date and they were not expired, so
don’t know why that was but please look into it. Tried the sweets, they were nice and fresh.

top_reviews.author: Imran Saeed Grewal
top_reviews.text: This store is a fantastic one-stop shop for authentic Pakistani and Indian products! The grocery section is well-stocked with a variety of spices, lentils, and snacks that are hard to find elsewhere. The Halal meat shop offers fresh, high-quality cuts, perfect for desi cooking. Their customer service is excellent, and the staff is always ready to help you find what you need. Whether you're looking for everyday essentials or ingredients for traditional dishes, this place has it all. Highly recommended for anyone in search of authentic Halal and South Asian products!"

name: Al Shorja International Market
specialties: Middle Eastern and South Asian food, spices, bakery, hot prepared food, fresh meats
formatted_address: 27727 Dequindre Rd, Madison Heights, MI 48071, USA

top_reviews.author: Jamie Sweet-Burrows
top_reviews.text: Convenient location! So happy I found this strip mall gem. I was attempting to make kafta for the first time & recipe called for ground sumac, dried mint, and fresh parsley leaves. I got all I needed in one stop. Friendly & welcoming environment.

top_reviews.author: Diana Peters
top_reviews.text: This new market is great! I went in for the first time recently and was really impressed with the helpful and nice staff, that greeted me with a smile. I tried some of the prepared food and it was delicious!!! The hummus and gaymur was some of the best I had ever had! They also have a nice selection of produce and good prices! Definitely coming back again and again!

top_reviews.author: DillenP03
top_reviews.text: This market is one of the best I’ve ever been. store workers are helpful and showed me some of the best bread I’ve ever tasted. Love this store. My mom frequently goes there and nothing I can say is bad about this place. Fresh, and quality. Recommend.

top_reviews.author: Zalal Uddin
top_reviews.text: Very friendly staff there. The owner and his son try to take care of you in any way they can. The only negative I can see is that selection of goods is limited. I hope they will improve

top_reviews.author: Lydia Yaldo
top_reviews.text: This is the best market in town! The customer service was top notch and not to mention, their produce is always fresh! Must go!

top_reviews.author: Amaan Rizvi
top_reviews.text: I've been here a few times the past few years, but honestly this year the quality has dropped. They had a buffet which was a switch from their menu. The food options were quite limited, which was a minus for me. Sadly the food was cold, and was quite mediocre. The best dish was their jalebi, but even that was ok.

top_reviews.author: Kamrun Nahar
top_reviews.text: Today I tried jalebi and haleem at Aladdin Sweets and Cafe, and everything was so good. The jalebi was fresh and sweet, and the haleem was rich, warm, and very tasty. Thank you to the Aladdin Sweets and Cafe team for making such delicious food for us.I really enjoyed it!

top_reviews.author: Md. Al-Amin
top_reviews.text: Premium quality Bangladeshi food in a clean and nicely maintained place. Spice level is optimum and so mouth watering. Staffs are super cordial. Provide plenty of options for Appetizers and Desserts. Can’t recommend more.

top_reviews.author: Aditi Roy
top_reviews.text: The service is horrible, orders take an extremely long time to be prepared. The food quality is very poor. This place used to offer good-quality Bengali snacks and meals, but that’s no longer the case. On top of that, the staff is very unfriendly. Overall, a very disappointing experience.

top_reviews.author: Adam Imam
top_reviews.text:
A horrible and disrespectful place and it happened right in front of the restaurant owner who himself were standing up front in the line and he allowed doing live video for a female customers who were standing in the line on the month of Ramadan 2026 (1st Day) ???

A simple blogger called himself a Journalist (Stupid) was doing video without asking a permission from female Customers. How is that possible in these days. Specially is USA …

A group of young girls were in the line and they were not feeling comfortable taking their live video. No respect for a female customers..

I had to raise my voice and step in and had to stopped that blogger doing video without female customer consent. That is illegal doing a live video inside any business place or restaurant without female customer consent. Again thats illegal.

I never recommend this kind of place as a family environment restaurant. Do not support this kind of restaurant in our community where female has no privacy inside the restaurant.

top_reviews.author: Rumana Begum
top_reviews.text: Ordered a good amount of food thinking it was going to be good. Ordered Chicken 65, 65 biryani, tandoori chicken and naan! VERYYY disappointed!! Really curious to know where the chef got his license from. Everything looked like it was made from scratch and aunty from Africa made it.

top_reviews.author: Yvette
top_reviews.text: We ordered the chicken wrap, fusca (pani puri), and a mango lassi.  Food was good and hot. I always wanted to stop in. Service was ok. He forgot my chuntneys. Restaurant could've been cleaner. Counter was nasty and cups on the floor. Atmosphere were all older guys sitting around, dark and gloomy on the inside. Not kid friendly. We waited about 15 minutes for our food. The food was the star of this experience.

top_reviews.author: Christopher Vinson
top_reviews.text:
Great little Buffett.  $7.99 for lunch!
They treated me kindly.
The food was fresh and hot.
Please keep in mind India, Pakistán, and Bangladesh were all one country at one period of time in history.
They share the sane cuisine!
I will be back.
Very romatic setting.

top_reviews.author: Nayem Official
top_reviews.text:
Here’s the honest experience with Bonoful

We ordered
1 beef biryani
1 chicken curry and 2 non

This order took around 35 minutes to make it ready. Then we didn’t get water on the table with the food. Had to ask her salad guess what they didn’t give us any.
Oh the slowest customer service I have ever seen in my life.

Honestly I don’t remember you guys to dine in here.
Honestly they don’t give a shitt about customers!!!

top_reviews.author: Tayefur Rahman
top_reviews.text: The worst food and service in the town. I have visited almost all local Indian food restaurants, but this experience was terrible. Just look at this beef biriyani picture!!! They put 4 large size bones on my plate. Oily and salty.. service was poor. Do nit waste your money there..

top_reviews.author: Redwan Karim Sony
top_reviews.text: I've been shopping here for the last 4 years. The store is clean and well-stocked with great Bangladeshi products. However, the restroom is locked with an 'Out of Order' sign, yet employees still use it. For a customer like me who drives hours to shop here, this feels unfair. Otherwise, it's a great place to shop.

top_reviews.author: Lauralyn Taylor
top_reviews.text:
The market is practically a mini Walmart with the range of products sold! It had pots, pans, rugs, nail polish, kitchen gadgets, glassware, and food that was American, Indian, Mexican and probably more. There was a nice selection of fresh vegetables and some fruits. There was also a refrigerated section with frozen vegetables, desserts, meals, and more. Milk, bread, butter, ghee, eggs... You name it, they had it (except thyme, lol, I didn't think they would have it, which makes sense, but since they had everything else, and I needed some, I looked 😊).

The store was clean and the employees were very helpful. I called before driving out (I live 10 miles away) and the employee was happy to tell me they carry fresh turmeric. When I arrived, he even took me back to where it is located.

top_reviews.author: Dewan Mahmud Ahmed
top_reviews.text: Billed for betel nuts but never received them. When I called immediately, the cashier responded irresponsibly and didn’t resolve it. Good selection, but poor service ruined the experience. Suggest no one to visit if it's not that much urgent.

top_reviews.author: •Syed Ali•
top_reviews.text: All of these foods were expired and were covered in a brown sticky thing. Please look at the expiration before buying these if you are health cautious.

top_reviews.author: Keith LaBeau
top_reviews.text: Love this store people are very welcoming great selection of stuff. Always have special discounts too. I highly recommend this place

top_reviews.author: Sharat Kumar
top_reviews.text: Good store, helpful staff, minimum $10 has to be billed for Credit card payment, all basic items available but the cost seems to be a little bit high compared to other stores(Desi curd), also the Indian snack items are not much available here.

top_reviews.author: Noorulann Sherwani
top_reviews.text:
I always went to get my groceries from now I will not go anymore because by accidentally I dropped glass bottle of garlic. I apologized it but she kept on saying you should get bigger cart, I said I only came to grab one item but because you had sale I picked up so many stuff.
she charged me for the broken bottle saying that I should grab bigger cart. I even told her that you’re loosing a customer but she didn’t care at all.
I have never seen place if you broke something by accidentally you have to pay.

top_reviews.author: roshan porob
top_reviews.text: They have finally got a long over due renovation. The place is bigger and now has more varieties. The fresh vegetable samosa they sell are among the best. Although one of the issues I faced almost every time still persists, missing price labels.

top_reviews.author: Geeta Madurkar
top_reviews.text: The most pathetic store with all EXPIRED ITEMS. Please check the dates and never buy any packaged goods from the store. I got a chickoo icecream container on 2/10/2026. When we opened it it had a thick layer of ice and beneath was all mold. The tub had expired in 2024.

top_reviews.author: Nand Kishore Gajarajan
top_reviews.text: Well stocked and well maintained place. Very helpful associates at the shop.  Fresh produce is also available in good quality and varieties 👍

specialties: Fresh veggies, spices & masala, desi snacks
formatted_address: 34703 Grand River Ave, Farmington, MI 48335, USA
hours: Monday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Tuesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Wednesday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Thursday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Friday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Saturday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM | Sunday: 10:00 AM – 9:00 PM

top_reviews.author: Navya
top_reviews.text:
I always come here for the chai, and the chicken pickle is absolutely fantastic. Their homemade samosas, puffs, and sweets are consistently the best—especially the onion samosas! The vegetables are always fresh, and they even make fresh chapati, which taste amazing.

The store is neatly arranged, clean, and well-organized. The staff is very friendly and helpful, and the prices are more affordable compared to other Indian stores. They also offer a wide range of grocery brands, which makes shopping so convenient.

I highly recommend this store to anyone looking for quality Indian groceries and delicious fresh snacks!

top_reviews.author: ashish reddy
top_reviews.text: A well maintained store. Cleanliness 💯. They extended their shelves and Organized superbly than before. They had almost every item like snacks, pickles, spices and lot more with multiple brands even the store look small. Very helpful employees. Especially the CHAI made by KK is awesome and the samosas are really good. Do visit NAMASTE GROCERIES 🤝

top_reviews.author: Mohana Puvvada
top_reviews.text: Best Indian Store to visit for fresh vegetables. Great finds. I Always find good quality products from India. Don’t miss out on their complementary chaii “THE BEST”.

top_reviews.author: Yashwanth M
top_reviews.text:
Always check the best before date before you buy.
There are some items on the shelf that has expired date and this item is kid’s horlicks drink.
I took this picture during September.

top_reviews.author: Karthik A
top_reviews.text: The experience was decent, but the place is quite small and not well-organized, making it a bit confusing to navigate. Additionally, the pricing was slightly higher compared to other stores. While it wasn’t a bad experience overall, better arrangements and more competitive pricing would improve it.
